definitions.text: To apply a force to (an object) so that it comes toward the person or thing applying the force.
examples: When I give the signal, pull the rope.
Pull your belt tight before starting off, will you?

definitions.text: To gather with the hand, or by drawing toward oneself; to pluck or pick (flowers, fruit, etc.).
examples: to pull fruit from a tree

definitions.text: Command used by a target shooter to request that the target be released/launched.
An act of pulling (applying force toward oneself).
examples: He gave the hair a sharp pull and it came out.
She took several pulls on her cigarette.

definitions.text: An attractive force which causes motion towards the source.
examples: The spaceship came under the pull of the gas giant.
iron fillings drawn by the pull of a magnet

definitions.text: An advantage over somebody; a means of influencing.
examples: The hypnotist exerted a pull over his patients.
